summary: Resolved five findings from the library PR's Greptile review: (1) scheduleSpecEqual timezone guard used && where || was needed, masking drift when one side omits the timezone; (2) run --only-new without --wait now fails fast instead of silently emitting an empty list; (3) added the missing FTS5 UPDATE trigger on pp_dataset_items so a direct row UPDATE keeps the search index in sync; (4) ab run launches both Actors concurrently via goroutines + WaitGroup instead of sequentially, for a fair comparison; (5) --max-cost is now enforced for any sample of at least one prior run (was silently bypassed below three), and enforcement was moved out of the --agent/--compact projection-display guard so agent invocations are still capped; a zero-history Actor prints an explicit unenforced-cap warning. Also removed the dead --max-cu flag and gave --offline real fail-fast-on-empty-store behavior.
rationale: Greptile P1/P2 findings on the novel-feature layer plus dead-code cleanup surfaced during pre-publish audit.
